## PADRĂ•ES DE PENSAMENTO

| PadrĂŁo | DescriĂ§ĂŁo | FrequĂŞncia |
|--------|-----------|------------|
| Experience-First Teaching | Always demonstrates (bad + good) before explaining theory | Every module |
| Self-Deprecating Humor | Uses humor about himself to build connection and lower guard | Constant |
| Student Before/After | Shows real student transformation as proof | Modules 2-5 |
| Musical Metaphors | Piano keys, legato/staccato, melody â€” voice IS music | Modules 1-3 |
| Framework Simplification | Takes complex models and distills to 3-5 steps max | Modules 3-4 |
| Default Identification | First step is always "identify what you're stuck in" | Throughout |

## EXPRESSĂ•ES TĂŤPICAS

- "It's not the best idea that wins. It's the best communicator."
- "If it distracts from the message, it's a problem."
- "Don't confuse unfamiliar with fake."
- "Your voice is an instrument with 88 keys."
- "Default = monotony = disengagement."
- "Make the horse thirsty first."
- "You're only as good as you can communicate."
- "Don't judge yourself on chapter one."
- "See you in the next video."
- "I hope you can see the power of..."
